description_long: This is a large commercial airplane with a cylindrical fuselage, featuring a cockpit at the front and a passenger cabin with rows of seats visible through a cutaway section. The aircraft has a pair of straight, low-mounted wings with four underwing engines, two on each side. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a classic tail design. The landing gear is visible beneath the wings and fuselage, configured in a tricycle arrangement. The exterior is primarily white and gray, with blue-tinted cockpit windows and small details such as passenger windows and doors. The interior is partially exposed, revealing seating and aisle arrangements typical of a passenger jet.
description: A large, white and gray commercial airplane with four underwing engines, a conventional tail, and a detailed passenger cabin visible through a cutaway section.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cockpit windows, nose, and the arrangement of seats inside the cabin.
description_view_1: Side cutaway view displaying the segmented fuselage, interior seating, and the alignment of the wings and engines.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the tail section, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the back rows of the passenger cabin.
description_view_3: Opposite side cutaway view, similar to view 1, showing the internal structure and seating from the other side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets; moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Four underwing turbofan engines, two per wing.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units under the wings and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multi-seat configuration and large blue-tinted windows.
